updates.tweet_text: @RobyArnoldi Yes 15 points full size is the max loss I'd ever take. If a larger stop is required though, you can half the trade size. A half size trade for example a 30 point stop would equal 15 on a full trade.
updates.notes: Reply explaining risk/position sizing rules. No price levels named, only stop-loss point distances (15, 30) used as math examples, not ES prices.

updates.tweet_text: @KevinJagiah That's how it is all on bears control days. My whole edge is based on this concept, ES almost never sells in straight lines. Its why "bears" won't typically make money even in downtrends because shorts are very fast and short lived then we squeeze. Nearly impossible to make money
updates.notes: Conversational reply explaining market structure/trading philosophy. No price levels named, no actionable entries, targets, or triggers mentioned.

updates.tweet_text: @OnTheHooch There's no timeline on it as I've always said, the longest I've seen was about 2 years but usually its within in a month or two. Probably 3-4 weeks average

updates.tweet_text: @alex_sidarenka We broke down a big support shelf causing a selloff, so bulls don't control again until that shelf recovers to invalidate it. It doesn't mean we cant bounce (the strongest bounces occur when bears control) but just don't expect them to last
